family_history.diabetes_family_history: Mother (type 2 diabetes diagnosed in her late 40s); maternal grandfather (type 2 diabetes, insulin dependent)
family_history.amputations_family_history: Maternal grandfather – below-knee amputation on right leg due to long-standing diabetic complications
family_history.cancer_family_history: Father – prostate cancer diagnosed at 67; maternal aunt – breast cancer diagnosed in early 50s, currently in remission
family_history.heart_disease_family_history: Father – coronary artery disease, had a stent placed at age 62; paternal grandfather – fatal heart attack at 72
family_history.strokes_family_history: Maternal grandmother – ischemic stroke at age 78 with partial recovery
family_history.high_blood_pressure_family_history: Both parents have hypertension controlled with medication; older brother recently started on blood pressure medication at age 45
family_history.other_family_history: Younger sister with hypothyroidism (Hashimoto’s). No known family history of autoimmune arthritis or clotting disorders.

social_history.job_description: Full-time senior systems analyst. Primarily desk-based work in an open-plan office environment. Spends most of the day (8–9 hours) sitting at a computer troubleshooting software issues, analyzing system logs, writing technical documentation, and joining video or phone meetings with clients and internal project teams. Occasionally walks between different departments and server rooms but overall has a fairly sedentary workday. Works Monday through Friday, typically from 8:30 AM to 5:30 PM, with occasional after-hours on-call support during major software deployments or outages.
social_history.recreational_activities_exercise: I try to stay moderately active despite having a desk job. Three evenings a week (usually Monday, Wednesday, and Friday) I walk around my neighborhood for about 30–40 minutes at a brisk pace, often with my dog. On Saturdays, if the weather is decent, I either go on a longer 60–90 minute walk on the local trail near White Rock Lake or ride my bike for about 10–15 miles at an easy pace. I used to play recreational basketball in a local league, but I stopped about two years ago because of recurring pain and stiffness in my right foot and ankle after games. I occasionally do basic bodyweight exercises at home (push-ups, planks, light stretching) but not on a strict schedule—maybe once or twice a week at most. I spend a fair amount of time on my feet on weekends doing yard work, grocery shopping, and house projects, but during the workweek I’m mostly sitting except for short breaks to stand up and stretch.
social_history.have_you_ever_smoked: Smoked lightly in college (about 3–5 cigarettes per day) for roughly two years, mostly socially at parties or when studying late. Quit completely in 2004 and have not smoked since. No use of cigars or smokeless tobacco.
social_history.type_alcoholic_beverages: Primarily beer and red wine, with an occasional mixed drink (whiskey and soda) at social events.
social_history.approx_amount_alcoholic_beverages: On average 3–5 standard drinks per week: typically one or two beers on Friday night, a glass of red wine with dinner on Saturday, and sometimes one additional drink during the week if out with coworkers or at a family gathering. Very rarely more than two drinks in a single evening.
social_history.recreational_drugs: N/A – I do not currently use recreational drugs. Experimented with marijuana a handful of times in college more than 20 years ago but have not used it or any other substances since.
social_history.surrogate_decision_maker_name: Spouse – Laura Marie Reyes (designated in advance directive and medical power of attorney)
